interview_evidence_quote: I reuse probably 70% of each proposal. The problem isn't writing — it's finding the right version.
Half the time I'm applying to things that aren't quite right for us and I know it going in.
Program descriptions are the most reused but the hardest to keep current.
SMART goals are in almost every federal grant we apply for. I write the same ones every year.
When Maria left — she took all the good language with her.
The budget narrative is the section I'm most likely to get wrong.
There are funders for BIPOC-led organizations that I often don't know about.
If a tool could just be a vault — a place where the good versions live — I would pay for that.

display_name: Theory of Change / Logic Model Narrative
interview_evidence_quote: Theory of change — I Googled that phrase the first time I saw it.
I've written the same equity statement 20 times in 20 slightly different ways.
Confirmed in NEA Our Town and collaborative foundation grants
Confirmed in HUD CDBG and USDA grants
Required by government grants and foundations with >$25K asks
We spent 3 days on a proposal — then realized we weren't eligible. LOI would have caught that.
Grant reports are nearly as burdensome as applications. Nobody thinks about this.
